title: Rewrapping bottle and its wrapping and unwrapping equipment and method
abstract: Rewrappable bottle and its wrapping and unwrapping equipment and method The rewrappable plastic bottle has a bottom (25) equipped with openings (26); the bottom can be flat or shifted from the bottle lower outskirt at least by 30mm. The equipment for the bottle wrapping and unwrapping consists of a conveyer (3.1) feeding unwrapped bottles (4.1) and a conveyer (3.2) removing wrapped plastic bottles (4.2). Furthermore, the equipment consists of a turntable (2) with fixed inner holder (7), vertical pneumatic cylinder-shaped handling unit (15) with vacuum pads (6), horizontal pneumatic handling unit (22) with self-clamping head (23), foil sleeves (13) magazine and heat chamber (10). The turntable (2) is equipped with vacuum pump (12) and the equipment includes a compressor (18). While wrapping the plastic bottle, the process is the following: the unwrapped bottle (4.1) is transferred to the inner holder (7), from the magazine (5) the foil sleeve (13) and by the mean of underpressure is slid on the unwrapped bottle (4.1). Afterwards, the bottle (4.1) is inserted inside the heat chamber (10) where the foil sleeve (13) is contracted and fixed in water-proof manner. The bottle (4.2) wrapped this way is transferred to the output conveyer (3.2). While unwrapping the bottle, the process steps are in reverse order, i.e., the used wrapped bottle (4.2) is transferred to the inner holder (7) and the foil is transferred by the mean of the air pressure into the refuse piping (19).
claims:
Claims 1. Rewrapping plastic bottle characterized in that its bottom (25) is equipped with at least 6 and no more than 12 openings (26) with minimum diameter of 1.5 mm and wall thickness 1.5 mm at least, whereas when wrapped, the bottle is outfitted with a foil with minimum thickness of 0.0015 mm on both outer and inner sides. 2. Rewrapping plastic bottle according to claim 1, characterized in that its bottom (25) is shifted from its lower outskirt by 30 mm at least. 3. Rewrapping plastic bottle according to claims 1 and 2, characterized in that it has a shape of cylinder, cuboid, carafe or heart. 4. Equipment for wrapping and unwrapping the bottle according to claims 1 to 3, characterized in that it consists of input conveyer (3.1) to feed a turntable (2) with either unwrapped bottles (4.1) or used wrapped bottles (4.2), output conveyer (3.2) for removal of either wrapped bottles (4.2) or unwrapped bottles (4.1) from the turntable (2) whereas the conveyers are fixed to a frame structure (20) and the turntable (2) is fixed to the frame (1), furthermore, both conveyers (3.1) and (3.2) and the turntable (2) being equipped, in their upper platform, with inner holders (7) for unwrapped bottles (4.1) and wrapped bottles (4.2) whereas a foil sleeves (13) magazine (5) with perforation is fixed on holders (14) to the frame (1) on its upper part, of vertical pneumatic cylindric handling unit (15) with vacuum pads (6), serving feeding the foil sleeves (13) from the magazine (5) and for the foil sleeve (13) sliding on an unwrapped bottle (4.1), being the turntable (2) on its lower part equipped with a vacuum pump (12) serving for the introduction of the overlapping part of the foil sleeve (13) into the unwrapped bottle cavity (4.1), a heat chamber (10) for the foil sleeve (13) contraction, fixed on the lateral side of the frame (1), a compressor (18) for pressure outward blowing of the used foil sleeve (16) from the used wrapped bottle (4.2) into the waste piping (19); furthermore, the equipment includes horizontal pneumatic handling unit (22) with self-clamping head (23) ensuring the transfer of either unwrapped bottles (4.1) or used wrapped bottles (4.2) from the input conveyer (3.1) to the turntable (2) and a horizontal pneumatic handling unit (11.2) with self- clamping head (11.1) ensuring the removal of either wrapped bottles (4.2) or unwrapped bottles (4.1) from the turntable (2) to the output conveyer (3.2). 5. The equipment according to claim 3 characterized in that the holders (7) for unwrapped bottles (4.1) and wrapped bottles (4.2) and holders (14) with fixed foil sleeves (13) magazine (5), vertical pneumatic cylinder-shaped handling unit (15) with vacuum pads (6) for foil sleeves (13) feeding from the magazine (5) and for the sliding of the foil sleeve (13) on an unwrapped bottle (4.1) and the heat chamber (10) for the foil sleeve (13) contraction are placed on the horizontal belt conveyer (8) and the vacuum pump (12) for the introduction of the overlapping section of the foil sleeve 

 (13) into the unwrapped bottle cavity (4.1) are on the lower part of the horizontal belt conveyer (8), being a compressor (18) for pressure outward blowing of the used foil sleeve (16) from the used wrapped bottle (4.2) into the waste piping (19) included to the equipment, which is furthermore including a horizontal pneumatic handling unit (22) with self-clamping head (23) ensuring the transfer of either unwrapped bottles (4.1) or used wrapped bottles (4.2) on the horizontal belt conveyer (8) from the input conveyer (3.1) and by the mean of horizontal pneumatic handling unit (11.2) with self- clamping head (11.1) ensuring the removal of either wrapped bottles (4.2) or unwrapped bottles (4.1) from the horizontal belt conveyer (8) to the output conveyer (3.2) . 6. Equipment according to claim 4 and 5 characterized in that the foil sleeves (13) on one of its sides, are blinded by melting, and their diameter is greater by 2 to 6 mm than the outer diameter of plastic bottle (4.1) and their length is equal to the sum of outer length of plastic bottle (4.1) and the plastic bottle (4.1) cavity height. 7. Equipment according to claims 4 to 6 characterized in that the foil sleeves (13) are made of polyethylene adapted for storage 10 to 150 Î¼Î¹Î· thick. 8. Equipment according to claim 4 and 5 characterized in that the conveyers (3.1) and (3.2) are equipped with electric drive (21) and the turntable (2) is equipped with electric drive (24). 9. Equipment according to claims 4 to 7 characterized in that the foil sleeve (13) is printed in its lower part. 10. Bottle wrapping method according to claims 4 to 9 characterized in that the unwrapped plastic bottle (4.1) is transferred by the mean of input conveyer (3.1) and horizontal pneumatic handling unit (22) with the self-clamping head (23) to the inner holder (7) placed on the turntable (2), in the position underneath the foil sleeves (13) magazine (5), subsequently taking a foil sleeve (13) from the magazine (5) by the mean of vacuum pads (6) and sliding it on the unwrapped bottle (4.1), then taking the overlapping section of the foil sleeve (13) into the unwrapped bottle cavity (4.1), resulting in the sliding of the foil sleeve (13) on the entire unwrapped bottle (4.1), introducing subsequently the wrapped bottle (4.2) inside the heat chamber (10) where air at the temperature between 75 and 85 Â°C is driven, which causes foil sleeve (13) contraction and its fixing in water-proof manner to both inner and outer sides of the wrapped bottle (4.1), the bottle wrapped this way being transferred from the inner holder (7) on the turntable (2) by the mean of horizontal pneumatic handling unit (11.2) with self-clamping head (23) on the output conveyer (3.2), while the process of wrapped bottle (4.2) unwrapping is characterized in that the wrapped bottle (4.2) is transferred, by the mean of horizontal pneumatic handling unit (22) with self- clamping head (23) from the input conveyer (3.1) to the inner holder (7) of the 

 turntable (2), and, by the mean of compressor (18) and air distribution located underneath the turntable (2), the air pression acting throughout the openings (26) on the wrapped bottle (4.2) bottom will release the used foil sleeve (16) from the bottle cavity (4.2) as well as from the outer side of the wrapped bottle (4.2) and remove the sleeve to the refuse piping (19) while the unwrapped bottle (4) without the foil is transferred from the inner holder (7) on the turntable (2) by the mean of horizontal pneumatic handling unit (11.2) with self-clamping head (11.1) on the output conveyer (3.2). 11. Method according to claims 4 to 10 characterized in that the unwrapped plastic bottle (4.1) is transferred either by the mean of input conveyer (3.1) and horizontal pneumatic handling unit (22) with the self-clamping head (23) or manually to the inner holder (7) placed on the horizontal belt conveyer (a), in the position underneath the foil sleeves (13) magazine (5), subsequently taking a foil sleeve (13) from the magazine (5) by the mean of vacuum pads (6) and sliding it on the unwrapped bottle (4.1), then taking the overlapping section of the foil sleeve (13) into the unwrapped bottle cavity (4.1) by the mean of the vacuum pump (12) via openings (26) on the unwrapped bottle (4.1) bottom (25), resulting in the sliding of the foil sleeve (13) on the entire unwrapped bottle (4.1), the mean of horizontal pneumatic handling unit (11.2) with self-clamping head (11.1) or manually, on the output conveyer (3.2), while the process of wrapped bottle (4.2) unwrapping is characterized in that the wrapped bottle (4.2) is transferred, either by the mean of input conveyer (3.1) and the horizontal pneumatic handling unit (11.2) with self-clamping head (11.1) or manually, to the inner
